# Paint To Hide Wiki — full content


## Paint To Hide Wiki — Roblox Codes, Hider & Seeker Guides (https://painttohide.com/)

Found a Paint To Hide code that would not redeem? It was probably for a different game. This wiki tracks the actual Paint To Hide! on Roblox — codes status, camouflage tips, seeker routes, and live stats — with sources you can check yourself.

### Paint To Hide codes: current status
There are no verified Paint To Hide codes right now — last checked July 4, 2026. The official Roblox page lists no codes button or reward system, and the developer's community page has announced none. Most results for this search actually cover Paint and Hide, a different Roblox game; its SUMMER! code will not redeem here. The codes page tracks the full status and how we verify it.

### Not the game you were looking for?
Paint To Hide! is one of six Roblox hide and seek games with nearly identical names. This wiki covers the one by Chabungus X Cache Flow: created May 19, 2026, rounds of up to 16 players, 50 million visits and counting. If you meant Paint and Hide (the one the codes sites cover) or Paint Or Seek, the comparison page sorts all six by creator, release date, and codes status.

### What is the Paint To Hide game?
Paint To Hide! is a Roblox hide and seek game where hiders paint themselves into the map instead of crouching behind cover. You pick a wall, a floor, or a prop, then paint yourself to match that surface and blend into the map — choose a pose and hold still while seekers sweep. Every hider a seeker catches joins the seeker team, so late rounds turn into a many-on-few hunt. The formula traces back to MECCHA CHAMELEON, the Steam game where you paint a plain white body to mimic the stage.

### How to play, in short
Paint, pose, survive the timer — or catch everyone before it runs out. Rounds are short and replayable, and the full guide covers round flow, controls, and the catch-and-convert rule.

#### Free cam for hiders (Shift + P)
Hiders get a free camera on Shift + P — it is on the game's own feature list. Detach the camera and inspect your paint job from the seeker's angle before you commit to a spot.

#### Private servers with free admin panels
Private servers come with a free admin panel, listed as a built-in perk on the official page. A private round with friends costs nothing extra to moderate.

Q: Is Paint To Hide the game with the SUMMER! code?
A: No. Codes sites list SUMMER! for Paint and Hide, a separate Roblox game with its own developer. Paint To Hide! by Chabungus X Cache Flow has no verified codes as of July 4, 2026, and codes never transfer between the two games.

Q: Who made Paint To Hide on Roblox?
A: Chabungus X Cache Flow, the Roblox group behind the game, released it on May 19, 2026. In its first seven weeks it passed 50 million visits and 75,000 favorites, with concurrent players around 35,000 to 40,000 on a typical day.

Q: Can you get banned for abusing the paint system?
A: Yes. The official description warns that paint-system abusers will be banned and tells players to report abuse to Roblox. Camouflage tricks within the rules are fine; exploiting paint mechanics to grief rounds is what the warning targets.


## Paint To Hide Codes — Real Status, No Copied Lists (2026) (https://painttohide.com/codes/)

Working Paint To Hide codes: none verified. Expired: none recorded. Last checked July 4, 2026. Before you retype a code from another site, read the one warning below — it will save you the trouble.

### Current Paint To Hide code status
No working codes exist for Paint To Hide! as of July 4, 2026. The official Roblox page shows no codes button, no rewards menu, and the description never mentions codes; the Chabungus X Cache Flow community page has announced none either. We re-check on a one-to-two-week cycle and update the date stamp above, so a stale date means we are due, not that codes appeared.

### The code you found is probably for Paint and Hide
SUMMER! and the other working codes you will find on codes sites belong to Paint and Hide — a different Roblox game whose name differs from this one by a single word. Those pages rank for Paint To Hide searches because search engines blur the two names, not because the codes work here. If you want that game's rewards, play that game; nothing you redeem there carries over.

### How redeeming will work if codes arrive
If the developer adds a code system, expect a Codes, Gift, or Rewards button in the lobby or settings — the standard Roblox pattern. We will post exact steps with screenshots the day a redeem box shows up in the game. Until the game itself shows one, treat any step-by-step list on other sites as unverified.

### How we check
We read the official game page, the developer's community page, and the in-game lobby, and we only mark a code active after an in-game redeem test. One honest limit: community-only drops can beat this page by a day or two. If you spot a code announcement we missed, the official community page is the place to verify it before typing anything in.

Q: Why are Paint To Hide codes not working?
A: Because the code you tried is almost certainly for Paint and Hide, a different game — or the game simply has no code system yet. Paint To Hide! has no codes button and no developer-announced codes as of July 4, 2026.

Q: When are new Paint To Hide codes released?
A: There is no release schedule, because there is no code system yet. The developer has not announced one on the game page or community page. Watch those two official sources; this page re-checks them every week or two.

Q: Do Paint and Hide codes work in Paint To Hide?
A: No. They are separate Roblox experiences by different developers, and Roblox codes only redeem inside the game that issued them. SUMMER! and similar codes reward coins in Paint and Hide only, and this page will not list anything as working until the developer ships a code system of its own.


## How to Play Paint To Hide — Roblox Rules & Controls (https://painttohide.com/how-to-play/)

One round of Paint To Hide teaches you the loop: hiders paint themselves into the map, seekers hunt, and everyone caught changes sides. Here is the full rule set before your first game.

### The round in 30 seconds
A Paint To Hide round splits up to 16 players into hiders and seekers. Hiders get a head start to paint themselves into the map and pick a pose; seekers then sweep the map to catch every hider before the timer ends. Any hider left standing when the clock runs out wins it for the hiding team.

### Playing hider: paint the surface, not your taste
Match the surface you are standing against — its color, then its texture. Press up against a wall or prop, copy its tone onto your body, and hold a pose that breaks your human outline. Movement is what gives hiders away, so pick a spot you can commit to for the whole round. The hiding spots guide goes deeper on camouflage and color matching.

### Playing seeker: catch and convert
Seekers win by finding every hider before time expires, and each catch makes it easier: caught hiders join the seeker team. That conversion rule means the first two minutes are the hardest — one good early catch starts a snowball. Look for outlines and color seams, not movement; a well-painted hider will not move at all.

### Controls and settings that matter
Two features from the official list change how you play.

#### Shift + P: the hider free cam
Shift + P detaches the camera while you hide. Fly it to where a seeker would stand and grade your own paint job — if you can spot yourself in two seconds, repaint.

#### Private servers and the free admin panel
Private servers include a free admin panel, per the official page. Custom rounds with friends need no extra purchase to set up and moderate.

Q: How many players are in a Paint To Hide round?
A: Up to 16 players per server, split between hiders and seekers. The Roblox game page lists 16 as the server size; exact team ratios are set by the game each round and can change with updates.

Q: What happens when a hider gets caught?
A: They switch sides and join the seeker team for the rest of the round. The official description states catches convert hiders into seekers, which is why rounds accelerate toward the end — the last hider standing often faces a double-digit seeker team.


## Paint To Hide Hiding Spots & Camouflage Tips (Roblox) (https://painttohide.com/hiding-spots/)

A good spot in Paint To Hide is not a hidden corner — it is any surface you can convincingly become. Here is how to choose one and paint it right.

### Pick the surface before the spot
Choose the surface you can copy, not the corner that feels safe. Busy textures — patterned walls, cluttered props, transition strips between two colors — hide outline mistakes that a flat, bright wall exposes. Corners feel safe but concentrate seeker attention, because every seeker checks them out of habit. Mid-wall positions in plain sight survive longer than the spot everyone thinks of first.

### Color matching and outline control
Match tone first, then worry about detail — a slightly wrong shade at the right brightness beats a perfect color that is too dark. Your silhouette is the bigger tell: pick a pose that merges your edges with the prop or wall line behind you. Two hiders on the same wall can have opposite odds purely on pose discipline. Numbers like exact paint values change with updates, so learn the principle, not a recipe.

### Grade yourself with the free cam
Press Shift + P, fly the camera to the doorway a seeker will enter from, and look for yourself. If your own eyes find you in a two-second scan, a seeker will too — repaint or reposition. This one habit is worth more than any list of secret spots, because it works on every map, including ones added after this guide.

### The line between clever and bannable
Creative camouflage is the whole game; exploiting the paint system is not. The developer's own page warns that paint-system abusers get banned and asks players to report them to Roblox. If a trick only works because it breaks the painting mechanic — rather than using it well — treat it as off-limits. This guide will never include exploit-based spots, and that is a feature, not a gap.

Q: What is the best hiding spot in Paint To Hide?
A: There is no fixed best spot — seekers learn popular ones within a day. The durable answer is any busy-textured surface away from corner clichés, painted to tone-match and checked with the Shift + P free cam from a seeker's approach angle.

Q: Does your paint have to match exactly?
A: No. Brightness and tone matter more than an exact color value, and a pose that hides your outline covers small color errors. Exact thresholds are the game's internals and shift with updates, so aim for close-and-still rather than perfect.


## Paint To Hide Seeker Guide — Find Every Hider (Roblox) (https://painttohide.com/seeker-guide/)

Paint To Hide seekers lose to the timer, not to hiders. A route, a reading habit, and one early catch are what beat it.

### Sweep in routes, not circles
Walk each zone once in a planned route instead of orbiting the map. Re-checking areas you already cleared is where seeker time dies; a route that touches every wall face and prop cluster exactly once leaves the timer for the hard finds. Start with the zones farthest from the seeker spawn — hiders assume you will clear the near rooms first and paint accordingly.

### What a bad paint job looks like
Look for seams, not shapes. A painted hider shows up as a tone patch that does not quite continue the wall: an edge where texture breaks, a shadow the surface should not cast, a prop with one detail too many. Scan surfaces at a shallow angle — outline errors flatten out when viewed head-on and pop from the side. Movement is a gift; most catches come from color seams.

### Snowball with catch-to-convert
Every catch in Paint To Hide adds a seeker, so play for one early find rather than a perfect sweep. Converted hiders often know exactly where their friends painted themselves — follow their first instinct move. The round's math flips fast: one catch at 16 players means the next sweep runs with double coverage.

Q: How do seekers win in Paint To Hide?
A: Find and catch every hider before the round timer ends. If even one painted hider survives the clock, the hiding team takes the round — so seekers play against time first and hiders second. Planned sweep routes and one early conversion are how seekers beat the clock.

Q: Do caught hiders help the seekers?
A: Yes. The official description says caught hiders join the seeker team, so each catch adds a searcher. Converted players also tend to know teammates' spots, which is why one early catch often decides a round.


## Paint To Hide vs Paint Or Seek vs Paint and Hide (Roblox) (https://painttohide.com/vs-paint-or-seek/)

Paint To Hide, Paint and Hide, Hide and Paint, Paint And SEEK, Paint Or Seek, Paint Hide and Seek — six different Roblox games, one blurred search result. This page tells them apart with checkable facts.

### The six games, side by side
Paint To Hide! (this wiki's game) is by Chabungus X Cache Flow, created May 19, 2026 — place 105281019603659. Paint and Hide! is place 133872250800930, the game the codes sites cover. Hide and Paint is by Dabloonian Empire, place 84133819568736. Paint And SEEK! (place 78724049937437, created April 2, 2026) is the oldest of the wave, and Paint Or Seek (place 85245205758607, created June 6, 2026) the youngest breakout. Paint Hide and Seek also runs as a mobile app off Roblox. Same camouflage idea, different developers, separate progress and codes.

### How to tell them apart in search results
Check the number in the Roblox URL, not the title. Every Roblox game link carries its place ID, and the six games' IDs never overlap — the list above is enough to verify you are opening the right one. Titles are decorated with emoji and event tags that change weekly, so the ID is the only stable name.

### Which one has codes?
Paint and Hide is the one with media-covered working codes — SUMMER! and similar coin codes, per the codes sites tracking it. Paint To Hide! has no verified codes as of July 4, 2026, and we found no codes coverage for the other four. Codes redeem only in the game that issued them, whatever a ranking page implies.

### They all trace back to one Steam game
MECCHA CHAMELEON on Steam popularized paint-to-camouflage hide and seek: plain white bodies, a stage to mimic, seekers on a timer. The Roblox wave adapts that formula with 16-player servers and round-based team conversion. Knowing the lineage helps searches too — guides for one game's painting fundamentals mostly transfer, while codes, maps, and updates never do.

Q: Are Paint To Hide and Paint and Hide the same game?
A: No. Paint To Hide! (place 105281019603659, by Chabungus X Cache Flow) and Paint and Hide! (place 133872250800930) are separate Roblox games by different developers. Codes, purchases, and progress do not transfer between them. Check the place ID in the Roblox URL before trusting any guide or code list.

Q: Which paint hide and seek game has codes?
A: Paint and Hide — codes sites list working coin codes like SUMMER! for it. Paint To Hide! has no verified codes as of July 4, 2026, and none of the other look-alike games have documented code systems we could verify.
